title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
West Virginia State Police Commissioner and Colonel Pickert testifies regarding the investigation into Andrew Van and the missing servant, Clinton. Pickert reports that Van was last seen on Thursday, December 24th, visiting Mrs. Rebecca Traub in Arroyo. Clinton was last seen that same afternoon between Arroyo and Pudding, where he purchased a bushel of potatoes from a farmer named Timothy Trainer; however, the potatoes were later found untouched at Van's house.

Casper Crocker, an auto repair shop owner from Wilton, Maine, is called to the witness stand. He testifies that at approximately eleven o'clock on Christmas Eve, a dark-skinned, clean-shaven man approached his shop and offered ten dollars to be driven to an intersection with New Kensington-Bridgeton Road in Arroyo. Crocker agrees to the request, and upon arriving at the destination, he observes the man walking away while limping on his left leg and carrying a shovel.

Following Crocker's testimony, Coroner Stapleton calls an elderly, disheveled man to the stand. The man has weathered skin, unkempt brown hair, and wears muddy shorts and a gray sweater. He carries a staff topped with a crudely carved snake-shaped statue. When asked to identify himself, the man does not provide a human name, but instead declares himself to be "Harakhti, the god of the midday sun. Ra-Harakti, which means the hawk!" This declaration leaves the courtroom in a state of stunned silence and fear.
